acceptance_criteria:
Acceptance
- Parsing files with duplicates or out-of-order entries fails with a specific WireError.
- Roundtrip tests verify canonical ordering and strict parsing.
- Existing valid stores continue to parse without changes.
description:
Problem
- wire::parse_state_full and friends accept any order and silently overwrite duplicate IDs (state) or duplicate dep keys.
- That means corrupted or hand-edited store files can silently drop data depending on line order.
- The canonical ordering invariants (sorted by BeadId / TombstoneKey / DepKey / NoteId) are not enforced at the parse boundary.

Impact
- Data loss can go unnoticed.
- Violates parse-don't-validate and the "ordering is a stability invariant" we rely on for deterministic diffs.
design:
Design
- Add a canonical JSONL parser for the git store that enforces strictly increasing order and uniqueness per file.
  - state.jsonl: BeadId strictly increasing, no duplicates.
  - tombstones.jsonl: BeadId (or TombstoneKey) strictly increasing, no duplicates.
  - notes.jsonl: (bead_id, note_id) strictly increasing, no duplicates.
  - deps.jsonl: DepKey entries strictly increasing, no duplicates.
- Return WireError::InvalidValue with file + line context on violations.
- Keep serialization order exactly matching these rules so roundtrip is deterministic.

Scatter fit
- Enforce ordering in one parser per file type; eliminate implicit reliance on serialize_* ordering.

Files
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/wire.rs
- crates/beads-rs/src/git/sync.rs (error reporting context)
title: git store JSONL: enforce canonical ordering + uniqueness
